## Summary

The issue requested adding an `onClick` event to the TabBar component so users can "update" the contents of the tab without additional controls.

### Changes Made:

1. **`src/components/tab-bar/tab-bar.tsx`**:
   - Added `onClick?: (key: string) => void` to `TabBarProps` type
   - Modified the item click handler to call `props.onClick?.(keyString)` before `setActiveKey(keyString)`

2. **`src/components/tab-bar/index.en.md`**:
   - Added documentation for the new `onClick` prop

3. **`src/components/tab-bar/index.zh.md`**:
   - Added Chinese documentation for the new `onClick` prop

### Behavior:

- `onChange`: Only fires when switching to a **different** tab (active key changes)
- `onClick`: Fires **every time** a tab is clicked, including clicking on the already active tab

This allows users to implement refresh/update functionality when clicking on the currently active tab, which was the requested feature.
